persons.arguments_against: Counter-hypothesis: Cooper is one of the commonest surnames in the corpus (75 staff officials, 41 biographies), so name and initials carry almost no weight, and the bio is a two-initial 'F. A.' form. This fails because three independent dimensions agree on a singular office: Director of Public Works, Ceylon is a unique single-holder post (bio 'dir. pub. wks., Ceylon, 1897' / staff list 'Director of Public Works' 1898-1913); the C.M.G. honour is shared (bio 'C.M.G. (1901)' / staff list carries 'C.M.G.' from 1905); and the retirement year matches exactly (bio 'ret., 1913' / staff list stint ends 1913). The combination of a unique office + matching honour + matching retirement year admits no realistic second F. A. Cooper.

evidence_cited.fact: F. A. Cooper | Director of Public Works
F. A. Cooper | Director of Public Works | C.M.G.

persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second F. A. Cooper holding the Ceylon Directorship of Public Works in this period, or that the bio's Director tenure ended well before 1913.

notes: Common surname offset by three agreeing independent dimensions: the singular office (Director of Public Works, Ceylon), the shared C.M.G. honour, and an exact retirement-year match (1913). Deterministic edition_cooccurrence not met, but the honour and singular-office agreement carry the case.
